issue_title: Do not rely on the `pwdLastSet` attribute when updating AD entries
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

We only update the status of the user, if enabled or disabled, if the `pwdLastSet` attribute is set to its corresponding LDAP entry in AD.

As a consequence, we do not show a proper error message when trying to update the user to indicate that the account cannot be updated due to the password not yet set. 

I'm not sure why we have this constraint so the proposal is to ignore the `pwdLastSet` attribute when updating the user and create the user as enabled just like when using vendors other than AD.

From a security perspective, the account is effectively not able to authenticate because no credential is set and AD user controls like `password_expired` will still work to force a password reset.

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/31456#issuecomment-2269846405.

### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When creating the user in AD, the user should be created just line any other user where the account is enabled by default.

### Actual behavior

When creating the user in AD, the user is disabled and you are forced to first set a password and then enable the user.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create an user in AD. The user is created as disabled.
* Try to enable the user

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not rely on the pwdLastSet attribute when updating AD entries

Closes #34467

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Renaming realm in UI broken
issue_body:

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In a freshly install using keycloak operator(26.0.4) in kubernetes, it is not possible to rename the "master" realm through the Admin UI.

issue_title: Non compliant OpenID Client Authentication when `client_secret_jwt` with PAR (Pushed Authorization Requests)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Endpoint `POST http://keycloak.local/realms/myrealm/protocol/openid-connect/ext/par/request` does not authenticate (returns 401) with `client_secret_jwt` when `client_assertion` and `request` keys are provided.

I.e. not compliant with [OpenID Connect PAR specs](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#ClientAuthentication) and [PAR's RFC-9126](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9126)


### Version

26.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A POST to `http://keycloak.local/realms/myrealm/protocol/openid-connect/ext/par/request` should return 201 with similar (values may vary) body response

```
{
  "request_uri": "urn:ietf:params:oauth:request_uri:2277ae07-1bd1-4d43-84dc-2adb1680a016",
  "expires_in": 60
}
```

when valid `request`, `client_assertion`, `client_assertion_type`, `scope`, `response_type` and `client_id` are provided.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak does not authenticate ANY request to `http://keycloak.local/realms/myrealm/protocol/openid-connect/ext/par/request` when valid `request`, `client_assertion`, `client_assertion_type`, `scope`, `response_type` and `client_id` are provided. Instead it returns:

```
status: 401
resp body:

{
  "error": "invalid_request",
  "error_description": "Authentication failed."
}
``` 

### How to Reproduce?

With any realm and client:

Perform a POST to endpoint `/realms/myrealm/protocol/openid-connect/ext/par/request` with content type as `x-www-form-urlencoded` and form with the following keys (defined in the [Pushed Authorization Requests RFC](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9126)) with valid values:

`request`, `client_assertion`, `client_assertion_type`, `scope`, `response_type` and `client_id`

### Anything else?

All the calls were done based on [RFC 9126](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9126) which refers to the Pushed Authorization Requests and [OpenID Client Authentication](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#ClientAuthentication) specification.
commit_message:
Failed to authenticate client with method client_secret_jwt when client has keys generated
closes #34547

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

core

### Describe the bug

Switching Keycloak's setting to "Email as Username" modifies existing usernames for users with custom usernames, replacing them with their email addresses. This unexpected behavior affects configurations where LDAP is set as the user storage. Keycloak updates the usernames in its own database, but when trying to update any attribute for a user with a modified username, it attempts to apply the username change to LDAP as well, resulting in an error.

### Version

26.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Enabling "Email as username" should only apply to new users created after the setting is changed, while existing users should retain their usernames unchanged, especially if LDAP is configured as the user store.

### Actual behavior

When "Email as Username" is enabled:
1. Keycloak automatically changes existing usernames to email addresses. This is returned by the API:
![Screenshot 2024-11-01 at 08 57 17](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/851def1d-b4b8-4896-9e4d-d84b3242114b)


2. Attempting to update a user attribute for a user whose username has changed will cause Keycloak to attempt to push the change to LDAP, resulting in an error.

```
2024-11-01 07:01:47,561 WARN  [org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.UserResource] (executor-thread-19) Could not update user!: org.keycloak.models.ModelException: Cannot change username if the realm is not configured to allow edit the usernames
	at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.mappers.UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper.checkDuplicateUsername(UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper.java:190)
	at org.keycloak.storage.ldap.mappers.UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper$1.setUsername(UserAttributeLDAPStorageMapper.java:244)
```


### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure LDAP as the user storage provider.
2. Create users with custom usernames (non-email).
3. Enable "Email as Username" in Keycloak settings.
4. Observe that usernames in Keycloak are changed to email addresses for existing users.
5. Attempt to update a user attribute for one of these affected users.

### Anything else?

Related discussions:
[Issue #33360](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/33360#issuecomment-2388371617)
[Issue #34167](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/34167) @ssilvert mentioned that enabling "Email as Username" should not change existing usernames, but in our case, usernames are indeed being altered, especially affecting LDAP synchronization.
commit_message:
Allow updating the username when registration as email is enabled during LDAP updates

Closes #34560

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Attributes missing in OrganizationRepresentation when using Admin REST API in Keycloak 26
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

After upgrading from Keycloak 25.0.6 to Keycloak 26.0.5 the attributes for the OrganizationRepresentation are missing when using the Admin REST API.

### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

OrganizationRepresentation in Keycloak 26 should contain attributes when using Admin REST API.

### Actual behavior

When using Keycloak 25 the Admin REST API the OrganizationRepresentation when calling the endpoint `/admin/realms/{realm}/organizations` looks like the following example: 

```
[
    {
        "id": "dca0ec5a-3440-476d-b41e-91730e8b2da9",
        "name": "test-01",
        "enabled": true,
        "description": "test-01",
        "attributes": {
            "custom-attribute": [
                "My Test Organization"
            ]
        },
        "domains": [
            {
                "name": "test-01.org",
                "verified": false
            }
        ]
    }
]
```

After an upgrade to any Keycloak 26.0.X version the response for the same request looks like this:
```
[
    {
        "id": "dca0ec5a-3440-476d-b41e-91730e8b2da9",
        "name": "test-01",
        "alias": "test-01",
        "enabled": true,
        "description": "test-01",
        "domains": [
            {
                "name": "test-01.org",
                "verified": false
            }
        ]
    }
]
```

### How to Reproduce?

- Running Keycloak in version 25.0.6
- Creating an organization with some attributes in the Admin UI
- Upgrading to Keycloak 26.0.5
- Calling `/admin/realms/{realm}/organizations` endpoint

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

authentication

### Describe the bug

The following exception occurs at the login attempt for the admin console of master realm, when the **user cache is disabled** (_spi-user-cache-default-enabled=false_).
`2024-11-04 08:32:43,879 +0100 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-1) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.UserCacheSession.isInvalid(String)" because "this.userCache" is null

	at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.conditional.ConditionalUserConfiguredAuthenticator.isConfiguredFor(ConditionalUserConfiguredAuthenticator.java:61)
	at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.conditional.ConditionalUserConfiguredAuthenticator.lambda$matchConditionInFlow$3(ConditionalUserConfiguredAuthenticator.java:38)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.MatchOps$1MatchSink.accept(Unknown Source)

	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.forEachWithCancel(Unknown Source)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyIntoWithCancel(Unknown Source)

	at java.base/java.util.stream.MatchOps$MatchOp.evaluateSequential(Unknown Source)

	at java.base/java.util.stream.MatchOps$1MatchSink.accept(Unknown Source)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList$ArrayListSpliterator.tryAdvance(Unknown Source)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.forEachWithCancel(Unknown Source)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyIntoWithCancel(Unknown Source)

	at java.base/java.util.stream.MatchOps$MatchOp.evaluateSequential(Unknown Source)

	at org.keycloak.authentication.DefaultAuthenticationFlow.isConditionalSubflowDisabled(DefaultAuthenticationFlow.java:354)

1. Configure `spi-user-cache-default-enabled=false`
2. Rebuild and start Keycloak
3. Try to login to master realm admin console

Workaround: Build Keycloak server with `features-disabled=organization`

issue_title: Client Protocol Mappers with non UUID ids cannot be edited
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In [MappingDetails.tsx](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/440624e39821689fa9364c6552a7ac2b8b604716/js/apps/admin-ui/src/client-scopes/details/MappingDetails.tsx#L49-L50) a check is made if the passed route parameter `mapperId` is a GUID or not.

If it is a GUID, we assume an existing Mapper is updated.
If it is not a GUID, we assume a new Mapper will be added.

Unfortunately, nothing stops you from passing a non-UUID as an id into the createMapper Request in `ProtocolMappersResource` or via SQL script. (we did this when we automated the creation of the "same" client in different realms)

The result is that it is not possible to edit those Mappers because the UI thinks it is a new one but then does not find it and throws an error.

```
react-dom.production.min.js:10 Error: Could not find the resource that you are looking for
    at We.z.config (MappingDetails.tsx:110:17)
```



### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Protocol Mapper without a GUID as ID can be edited.

### Actual behavior

Protocol Mapper without a GUID as ID can not be edited.

### How to Reproduce?

1) Add a Protocol Mapper via REST endpoint that has not a valid GUID as id 
```
POST https://kc-host/auth/admin/realms/yyy/clients/2815b512-f45a-4e03-826a-23f6c8845a6b/protocol-mappers/models
Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "id": "not-a-guid",
  "protocol": "openid-connect",
  "protocolMapper": "oidc-audience-mapper",
  "name": "test-mapper",
  "config": {
    "included.client.audience": "account",
    "included.custom.audience": "",
    "id.token.claim": "false",
    "access.token.claim": "true",
    "introspection.token.claim": "true"
  }
}
```

2) Try to edit the Protocol Mapper in the UI

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: KC_CACHE_EMBEDDED_MTLS_ENABLED is ignored
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

The environment variable `KC_CACHE_EMBEDDED_MTLS_ENABLED` is always evaluating to its default value, `false`.

In the `CacheManagerFactory` class, the condition

```
Configuration.isTrue(CachingOptions.CACHE_EMBEDDED_MTLS_ENABLED_PROPERTY)
```

is always set to false.

The problem is that the `kc.` header, known as `NS_KEYCLOAK_PREFIX`, isn't being added to the string when being evaluated. A workaround for this problem is to use `CACHE_EMBEDDED_MTLS_ENABLED`, without the `KC_` prefix.

To fix this, there are two potential solutions.

1. Add the `NS_KEYCLOAK_PREFIX` to the Configuration.isTrue(String) method.
Change
```
public static boolean isTrue(String propertyName) {
        return getOptionalBooleanValue(propertyName).orElse(false);
    }
```
to
```
public static boolean isTrue(String propertyName) {
        return getOptionalBooleanValue(NS_KEYCLOAK_PREFIX + propertyName).orElse(false);
    }
```

2. Use Configuration.isTrue(Option<Boolean>) instead in `CacheManagerFactory`.
Change 
```
if (Configuration.isTrue(CachingOptions.CACHE_EMBEDDED_MTLS_ENABLED_PROPERTY)) {
            ...
        }
```
to
```
if (Configuration.isTrue(CachingOptions.CACHE_EMBEDDED_MTLS_ENABLED)) {
            ...
        }
```

### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

By setting `KC_CACHE_EMBEDDED_MTLS_ENABLED` to `true`, the log message `MTLS enabled for communications for embedded caches` should appear

### Actual behavior

Since it's always defaulting to `false`, the log message doesn't appear and TLS doesn't get properly set up.

### How to Reproduce?

Set the environment variable `KC_CACHE_EMBEDDED_MTLS_ENABLED` to `true`, and the MTLS enabled logging message will not appear.

### Anything else?

- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/storage/infinispan/CacheManagerFactory.java#L404
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/configuration/Configuration.java#L56
commit_message:

commit_message:
Fix(admin-ui): key status filtering issue in KeysListTab (#34721)

Support customer reports that keys with 'Active' set to 'off' still appear in the Active keys list in the Admin console under Realms settings -> Keys -> Keys list. The root cause was identified as the filtering logic, which does not apply status-based filtering for the first item in FILTER_OPTIONS ('ACTIVE'). This commit corrects the filtering logic to properly exclude keys based on the 'Active' status selection.

Closes #34675

Signed-off-by: Charley <charley.geoffroy@protonmail.com>

issue_title: [Admin UI] [Create resource-based permission] Resource input is disabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

# Background

The clients with the enabled fine-grained authorization have the **Authority** tab, where the **resources** and the accompanying **policies** and **permissions** can be defined.

The admin can create a resource-based permission for the given resource in the table simply by clicking the "Create permission" next to the resource of interest.

![377258023-2c99fa4a-2182-499d-b483-56657a8fb641](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aa98e1c3-592b-428e-a383-988db6173df8)

After clicking on the **Create permission** button, the user is redirected to the **Create resource-based permission** form with the pre-filled data, based on the selected resource.

e.g.

![377258325-b2dfccb3-0d46-47c8-8d7b-2fdfcc6ee873](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/abe4700e-569c-4a48-874b-2da5dddbf26f)

# Problem

The **Resource** input is disabled. It's not possible to choose a different resource or add an another one.

However, when the permission is being created via the **Authorization** -> **Permissions** -> **Create permission** -> **Create resource-based permission**, the **Resource** input is editable and selecting multiple resources is possible.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d61fb0b4-7fc8-43d2-9f5d-a2323ec7b304)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5375e9d4-fa12-4d56-9167-f0f54523201b)


### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The **Resource** input is not disabled in any of the described scenarios. The admin user can change resource, add more resources via the dropdown input freely.

### Actual behavior

The **Resource** input is disabled. The admin user can't change resource, nor can't the admin add more resource(s).

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start up **Keycloak**
2. Navigate to **Clients**, select **Import client**
3. Upload the demo client with the **Authorization** enabled, e.g. : https://gist.github.com/rh-/e0ddf9d94f48efc2812c5c7612036264
4. Navigate to the newly imported client **demo-resource-permission-selection** -> **Authorization** -> **Resources**
5. Click **Create permission** next to a resource


### Anything else?

This worked as expected in **25.0.6**.
The problem first appeared in **26.0.0** and it remains through the **26.0.5**.
commit_message:
no longer disable when resource is pre selected (#34702)

as you can add more resources

fixes: #34678

Signed-off-by: Erik Jan de Wit <erikjan.dewit@gmail.com>

authorization-services

after upgrading keycloak to version 25 from a previous version (upgraded from 11 to 16 then 25), the previously valid cookies sent by browser cause the following exception:

issue_title: Do not remove users in LDAP when queries return an empty result
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

When using LDAP, users are deleted from the local storage when the LDAP query returns an empty result set. 

For some use cases, it might make sense to keep local users even though they do not have a corresponding LDAP entry or when the LDAP configuration changes often and requires administrators to constantly update the provider configuration.

We might consider introducing a setting to the LDAP provider configuration to disable the removal of users. This setting should be used with cautious and only if administrators are aware of having users in the local database have precedence over the current state in the LDAP server.

Perhaps a valid use case is when migrating users from a legacy LDAP storage where you want the local storage as the single source of truth and up-to-date user storage. 

### Version

26.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Prioritize users from local storage over LDAP provider(s).

### Actual behavior

Prioritize users from LDAP provider(s) over local storage.

### How to Reproduce?

N/A

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not remove users in LDAP when queries return an empty result

closes #34764

issue_title: Invalid flag for addDefaultRequiredActions
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

In UserCacheSession.java:807 it calls the delegate with:
UserModel user = getDelegate().addUser(realm, id, username, addDefaultRoles, addDefaultRoles);

Should this be:
UserModel user = getDelegate().addUser(realm, id, username, addDefaultRoles, addDefaultRequiredActions);

to match the method signature?

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The delegate should be called with the boolean flag to create the required actions.

### Actual behavior

The delegate should is called with the boolean flag to create the default roles.

### How to Reproduce?

issue_title: Realm export does not format user data correctly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/cli

### Describe the bug

The user data file that is generated from my realm CLI export is not in a valid format for Docker. It contains a few extra fields, which cause it to be skipped when building a Docker container for Keycloak.

### Version

26.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When I export my realm file with the command

bin/kc.bat export --dir /auth-server --realm Realm-Name

the generated Realm-Name-users-0.json file is user data in the format:
[ {
    ...
  } ]

### Actual behavior

When I export my realm file with the command

bin/kc.bat export --dir /auth-server --realm Realm-Name

the generated Realm-Name-users-0.json file is user data in the format:

{
  "realm" : "Employee-Management-Service",
  "users" : [ {
    ...
  } ]
}

and that realm tag causes the file to be skipped when I build and run a Docker container.

### How to Reproduce?

**(Windows, Git Bash) - Create a realm named 'Realm-Name' and a user within that realm.**

**Run the export command from Git Bash:**

> bin/kc.bat export --dir C:/temp --realm Realm-Name

**Create a Dockerfile:**

FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26.0.4

COPY keycloak-26.0.4/conf/server.p12 /opt/keycloak/conf/server.p12
COPY keycloak-26.0.4/conf/keycloak.conf /opt/keycloak/conf/keycloak.conf
COPY Employee-Management-Service-realm.json /opt/keycloak/data/import/realm.json
COPY Employee-Management-Service-users-0.json /opt/keycloak/data/import/users-0.json

ENV KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=auth-admin
ENV KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=changeit
ENV KC_IMPORT=/opt/keycloak/data/import

EXPOSE 9883

ENTRYPOINT ["/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh", "start", "--import-realm"]

**Trigger from a docker-compose.yml file:**

services:
  ...
  auth-db:
    image: postgres:latest
    container_name: auth-db
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: changeitdb
      POSTGRES_DB: authorization-db
    ports:
      - "5433:5432"

  keycloak-server:
    build:
      context: ./authorization-server
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: keycloak-server
    image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest
    environment:
      DB_VENDOR: postgres
      DB_ADDR: auth-db
      DB_PORT: 5432
      DB_DATABASE: authorization-db
      DB_USER: postgres
      DB_PASSWORD: changeitdb
      DATABASE_URL: jdbc:postgresql://auth-db:5433/authorization-db
      DATABASE_USERNAME: postgres
      DATABASE_PASSWORD: changeitdb
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: auth-admin
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: changeit
    depends_on:
      - auth-db
    ports:
      - "9880:9880"
    volumes:
      - ./authorization-server/keycloak-26.0.4/conf:/opt/keycloak/conf
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "pg_isready", "-U", "postgres", "-d", "authorization-db", "-h", "auth-db", "-p", "5433"]
      interval: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s
      timeout: 10s

### Anything else?

Doesn't seem like docker-compose.yml is formatted correctly in the preview window, might be worth it to run that through an auto formatting tool.

Logs:

[org.keycloak.services] (main) KC-SERVICES0050: Initializing master realm
Full importing from file /opt/keycloak/bin/../data/import/realm.json
Realm 'Employee-Management-Service' imported
Full importing from file /opt/keycloak/bin/../data/import/users-0.json
Realm 'Employee-Management-Service' already exists. Import skipped

issue_title: Log handler specific log levels support only lower-case levels
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Log handler specific log levels support only lower-case levels (`info` for example), while the global level supports upper case (INFO or info).

This is a bit confusing as the syntax differs between the two, and also the [documented log levels](https://www.keycloak.org/server/logging#_log_levels) are all in upper case.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Both `--log-syslog-level=INFO` and `--log-syslog-level=info` should work just like for `--log-level`.

### Actual behavior

The following error message is displayed:
```
Invalid value for option '--log-syslog-level': INFO. Expected values are: off, fatal, error, warn, info, debug, trace, all
```

### How to Reproduce?

`kc.sh start-dev --log-syslog-level=INFO`

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Keycloak needs to return "invalid_request" from Token Endpoint if a token or refresh request lacks DPOP proof
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

According to [Section 5.2 of RFC 6749](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-5.2), Keycloak needs to return "invalid_request" or "invalid_grant" from Token Endpoint without DPOP proof.
However, the current Keycloak returns "invalid_dpop_proof".

### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A client that is required to send DPoP Proof sends a token request or token refresh request without DPoP Proof.
We expect that Keycloak returns an error "invalid_request"

### Actual behavior

Keycloak returns an error "invalid_dpop_proof".

### How to Reproduce?

A client that is required to send DPoP Proof sends a token request or token refresh request without DPoP Proof.


### Anything else?

This issue need to be resolved to pass the latest version of FAPI 2.0 specification.
https://github.com/keycloak/kc-sig-fapi/issues/649
https://github.com/keycloak/kc-sig-fapi/issues/655
